threads.content:
AI can generate 50 website layouts in 5 minutes.

Not one of them will make you feel anything.

This is the creative gap nobody wants to discuss. AI design tools have gotten fast, cheap, and technically competent. What they haven't gotten is taste.

What's the last website that genuinely surprised you?

A creative director told me she stopped giving her team AI tools for 2 weeks.

The reason? They'd stopped pushing back on bad ideas. Every design review turned into "let me ask the AI" instead of "here's why this doesn't work." The critical thinking muscle had atrophied in 3 months.

Are you exercising judgment or just accepting output?

instagram.caption:
The internet is full of boring websites. Recent (formerly Godly) curates the ones that aren't. Use it to reset your taste. Day 13 of 30.

#ai #artificialintelligence #tech #developer #startup #coding #automation #futureofwork

Save this for later. Follow @founderswing for daily AI breakdowns.

What do you think? Drop your take below.

tweets:
AI can generate 50 website layouts in 5 minutes.

A thread 🧵👇

Taste is the ability to choose between good and great. It comes from seeing thousands of examples, developing preferences, and understanding cultural context. It's accumulated slowly and can't be downloaded.
Right now, AI optimizes for "correct." Clean hierarchy, proper contrast, responsive grids. All technically sound. All completely forgettable.
The designers winning in 2026 are using AI for speed and their own taste for direction. They generate 50 options in 5 minutes, then spend 2 hours selecting and refining the one that has soul.

The danger isn't that AI replaces creative judgment. The danger is that teams stop exercising theirs. When you accept the first AI output because it's "good enough," you're training yourself out of the exact skill that makes you irreplaceable.

Use AI to produce volume. Use your taste to make choices.
What's the last website that genuinely surprised you?

twitter.content: The internet is full of boring websites
